title: Oeosporangium viride
taxonomy: Oeosporangium viride is classified within the family Aspleniaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the maidenhair spleenworts. It belongs to the genus Oeosporangium and is further classified as a species of this genus. This classification places Oeosporangium viride as a member of the class Polypodiopsida, which includes most types of ferns. The kingdom to which Oeosporangium viride belongs is Plantae, encompassing all types of plants. Oeosporangium viride's classification can be summarized as: Oeosporangium viride, Kingdom: Plantae, Class: Polypodiopsida, Family: Aspleniaceae, Genus: Oeosporangium.

intro: Oeosporangium viride is a type of lichenized fungus, specifically a species of Ascomycota, known for its unique symbiotic relationship with algae. The plant is reported to have various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat conditions such as fever, coughs, and skin irritations. Bioactive constituents of Oeosporangium viride include compounds like usnic acid, evernic acid, and barbatic acid, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Oeosporangium viride is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin conditions.
